g.text: public.upgrade row lifecycle — discovery, execution, and recovery decision tree
The only way to know these paths actually work is to RUN them — the problem is too hard
to reason out. commit -> push -> CI builds the per-commit image -> run on a real VM ->
observe -> iterate. This diagram is the map (each case + its TEST coverage); the run is
old-sb-upgrading (before booting the new binary) → roll back trivially (nothing changed) [auto]
exhausts → no longer transient → roll back → rolled_back
rolled_back — rollback succeeded; healthy on old version [auto]
(in_progress loop) — [HUMAN: unknown] waits for a person
§5 Park (STATBUS-046 — already-at-new forward, can't roll back)
Forward, after booting the new binary, keeps failing already-at-new
(integrators may have written past the maintenance-off point → rollback
forbidden). The bound is a crash-resume DEATH BUDGET (3 process deaths;
recovery_attempts counted at attempt START so a death self-counts, deaths =
attempts-1), plus same-step-twice → deterministic-hang park, plus B/C
failures → park on first. On terminal:
data-safe/cannot-reach-new → roll back (039 direction; 110 makes it byte-safe)
A parked row is SKIPPED by every automatic resume (alive-idle, siren once).
§6 STATBUS-145 — the delta leaves the boot window; park shrinks
boot-migrate now catches the schema up ONLY to the daemon floor; the upgrade
DELTA runs once at the applyPostSwap migrate step. So on a resume the delta is
PENDING → observed state reads cannot-reach-new (Behind) → the disposition is
ROLLBACK, not park, for EVERY pre-delta step failure (config, pull, db-up,
reconnect, AND deterministic B/C — §3/§5 park-on-first now applies only
at-target). PARK shrinks to exactly two cases: (a) repeated process deaths at
pre-delta steps (RecoveryBudgetGuard, canRollBack=false), and (b) a POST-delta
at-target failure (the health-check window). Upgrades are effectively ATOMIC:
apply the delta once, restore on any interruption.
UN-PARK = the two deliberate operator triggers only (re-schedule / ./sb
install) — each buys exactly ONE fresh attempt with a reset budget.
chk_upgrade_state_attributes enforces the timestamp columns per state
(e.g. completed ⇒ completed_at NOT NULL, error NULL; rolled_back ⇒
rolled_back_at + error NOT NULL; failed ⇒ error NOT NULL, no rolled_back_at).
rolled_back = [auto] snapshot restored, healthy at the old version.
failed = [HUMAN: restore-broke] rollback's restore itself broke — box cannot reach runnable state.
(in_progress loop) = [HUMAN: unknown] unrecognised phase or error — waits for a person.
stays in_progress + loud, waiting for a person
already-at-new forward: budget (3 process deaths) exhausted, same-step-twice,
or a B/C failure — PARK (recovery_parked_at + reason).
Un-park ONLY via a deliberate operator trigger (re-schedule / ./sb install)
OR persistent error (zero retries) → roll back
